senses.definition: to be immediately noticeable or strikingly obvious

examples.x: The spelling errors in the document jumped out at me.
Her enthusiasm and passion for the project jumped out at the interview panel.
The discrepancies in the data jumped out at the researcher.
The unique architectural features of the building jump out at visitors.
His talent for music jumped out at his teachers from a young age.

senses.definition: to immediately captures someone's attention in a sudden and striking manner

examples.x: The brightly colored poster immediately leaps out at me from across the busy street.
The sudden flash of lightning leapt out at us from the dark sky.
I'm sure the new addition to our living room will leap out at our guests when they see it for the first time.
The contrast between the black and white images will leap out at the viewers.
The bold headline of the newspaper article will leap out at passersby.

senses.definition: to unfold in a particular way

examples.x: Let's wait and see how the situation plays out before making a decision.
We're not sure what her strategy is, but we're curious to see it play out.
How do you think the negotiations will play out?

senses.definition: to show something to someone by pointing one's finger toward it

examples.x: He pointed out the star constellation to his children.
At the museum, the guide pointed out the most significant artifacts.
During the hike, he pointed out interesting landmarks along the trail.
When we visited the art gallery, she pointed out her favorite paintings.
As we explored the cave, bats popped out from hidden crevices.
I was surprised when her name popped out during the conversation.
The sun popped out from behind the clouds.
As I walked by the tree, a squirrel popped out from a branch.

senses.definition: to read a text or content aloud, articulating the words for others to hear

examples.x: The teacher asked a student to read out the poem in front of the class.
He read out the news headlines to the group, keeping them updated.
During the play, she had to read out her lines with confidence.

senses.definition: to focus on a particular person or thing from a group in either a positive or negative manner

examples.x: The teacher singled out the student for their outstanding essay.
The teacher didn't want to single out any student, so she addressed the entire class about the importance of completing assignments on time.
The coach decided not to single out any player for the team's loss, emphasizing the collective effort needed for improvement.
The art critic chose to single out one painting in the gallery, praising its unique perspective and vibrant colors.
In the team meeting, the manager made it a point to single out Sarah for her outstanding leadership during the project.

senses.definition: to be prominent and easily noticeable

examples.x: The glowing neon sign made the cafe stand out among the other businesses on the street.
Among the many candidates, his qualifications and experience made him stand out during the job interview.
The unique architecture of the building made it stand out in the city skyline.
The talented singer's powerful voice allowed her to stand out among the competitors in the singing competition.
Her colorful dress made her stand out in the crowd of people wearing neutral tones.

senses.definition: to be easily noticed, often due to being different from the surrounding elements

examples.x: The tall tower stuck out on the skyline, drawing the eyes of all who passed by.
The performer's eccentric costume and dance moves made them stick out on the stage.
The neon sign stuck out on the dark street, guiding people to the open restaurant.
The sparkling diamond necklace stuck out on the woman's neck, catching everyone's attention.
The brightly colored hair of the teenager made her stick out in the conservative school environment.

senses.definition: to emerge as a particular outcome
to loudly shout something, often suddenly and with the intention of grabbing someone's attention

examples.x: The event organizer yelled out last-minute changes to the schedule to ensure everyone was informed.
The captain of the ship yelled out commands to the crew during the storm.
The director yelled out cues to the actors during the live performance.
